architecture.summary: LaRS is an unsupervised in-context learning (ICL) example selection framework for chain-of-thought prompting. It learns a latent space representation of rationales (intermediate reasoning steps) via unsupervised learning to extract abstract reasoning skills. Concurrently, it trains a reasoning policy that maps a given question to the required reasoning skill in this latent space. During inference, ICL examples are selected by aligning the reasoning skills between candidate examples and the input question, eliminating the need for manual skill description or auxiliary LLM inference.

biblio.title: LaRS: Latent Reasoning Skills for Chain-of-Thought Reasoning

claims.statement: LaRS consistently outperforms state-of-the-art skill-based selection methods
LaRS processes example banks four times faster than baseline methods
LaRS reduces LLM inferences during the selection stage by half
LaRS shows greater robustness compared to SOTA skill-based selection methods

baseline.name: existing skill-based methods requiring human experts or pre-trained LLMs

claims.statement: LaRS eliminates the need for auxiliary LLM inference or manual prompt design required by existing methods
